Home / Alternatives to Mixpanel / Mixpanel vs Umami

Mixpanel vs Umami

A side-by-side look at Mixpanel (the paid SaaS) and Umami (the open source alternative). Use this page to decide if the switch fits your team and workflow.

Mixpanel Umami
Tagline Product analytics and funnels. Self-hosted privacy-focused analytics.
License Proprietary SaaS MIT
Pricing Free up to 20M events/month; Growth from $28/month. Free to self-host · optional paid hosted plan
Self-host option No Yes — difficulty 2/5
Hosted cloud available Yes (only option) Yes
Desktop apps Varies by product Web only
Mobile apps Official apps typically available None official
Ad slot — between tables

Best for

Self-hosted event tracking on your own database.

Umami strengths

  • Easy docker deploy with Postgres/MySQL.
  • Clean UI and fast dashboard.
  • Multi-website support out of the box.

Umami weaknesses

  • Less detailed than Matomo for heavy analysis.
  • Smaller integration ecosystem.
  • No built-in alerting.

What's the catch with Mixpanel?

  • Pricing grows steeply beyond free tier.
  • No self-hosted option.
  • Overkill for simple web traffic.

Still unsure?

Check the full list of alternatives to Mixpanel: see Mixpanel alternatives, or learn more about Umami on its project page.

OpenInstead — open source alternatives to the SaaS you already use. Updated April 2026.
Disclaimer: licensing, features and pricing change. Verify each tool's current status on its official site before committing.
